text:
Athletes fromNorth Koreaand South Korea joined together for a remarkable selfie after sharing the podium at theOlympic GamesinParis.

North Korea claimed the silver medal in the mixed doubles table tennis event on Wednesday with South Korea picking up bronze.Chinatopped the podium, picking up gold.

Following the medal ceremony, South Korea’s Lim Jong-hoon took out his phone to take a picture with representatives of all three teams in a beautiful moment of sporting unity.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

Ri Jong Sik and Kim Kum Yong of North Korea, Shin Yu-bin of South Korea and Chinese pair Wang Chuqin and Sun Yingsha were all beaming as Lim took the picture.

‘I congratulated them when they were introduced as Silver medallists,’ Lim told Korean media after the award ceremony.

It was a rare showing of cross-border unity amid rising tensions between the two countries. In January, North Korea’s leader Kim Jong Undescribed South Korea as a ‘primary foe and invariable principal enemy’amid escalating tensions between the two countries.

Athletes have previously been discouraged from taking shots of themselves on the podium but that has changed in Paris this summer with ‘The Victory Selfie’ initiative being promoted by organisers.

Olympic organisers were forced to issue an apology following a blunder during the opening ceremony in Paris where theSouth Korean team was mistakenly introduced as North Korea.

A the boat carrying the South Korean delegation passed along the River Seine, the athletes on board were introduced as the ‘Democratic People’s Republic of Korea’ – the official name of North Korea.

We apologise wholeheartedly,’ IOC spokesman Mark Adams said during a press briefing Saturday.

‘An operational mistake was made. We can only apologise, in an evening of so many moving parts, that this mistake was made.’

title: North Korea and South Korea athletes pose for Olympics selfie | Metro News
description: Athletes from North Korea and South Korea posed for a selfie together after sharing the table tennis podium at the Olympic Games in Paris.
